subject: Re: Tilt series deselected with change_selection still processed
body:
Hi Spencer,

You're right, this is a bug. I traced through the code and can confirm: change_selection correctly sets UnselectManual="True" in the tilt series XML, but no downstream commands (ts_ctf, ts_aretomo, etc.) check that flag before processing. The root cause is in BaseOptions.cs -- all files matching the input pattern are loaded into InputSeries without filtering by UnselectManual. The processing loop then iterates over every item unconditionally.

I've filed a GitHub issue to track the fix.

For excluding entire tilt series, your --input_data include.txt workaround is the most reliable approach right now. Deleting the XMLs from warp_tiltseries/ as Hamid suggested also works.

For excluding individual tilts within a series: change_selection DOES work at the frame series level. You can deselect bad frames using --settings warp_frameseries.settings, then re-run ts_import. It checks UnselectManual on each frame series and excludes deselected frames from the assembled tilt series (ImportTiltseries.cs, around line 287). This is the most reliable approach for individual tilt exclusion.

The UseTilt field in the tilt series XML is the other mechanism. Each entry corresponds to one tilt, and setting it to False should exclude that tilt. However, be aware that excluded tilts may still be weighted at 0.0001 rather than truly zero in some reconstruction paths. For reliable exclusion, re-importing with the bad frames deselected at the frame series level is the safer route. The mismatch errors you saw with Hamid's script are likely because the number of UseTilt entries must match the number of tilts listed in MoviePath within the same XML.
